sentences.sentence: I am looking at a very thick twisted trunk, rising to medium height, at which point appears a stumpy canopy of spiky needles.
sentences.translation: 我正看着一根非常粗壮而扭曲的树干，它向上长到中等高度，那里顶着一簇矮壮、长满尖针叶的树冠。

structure.text: I am looking at a very thick twisted trunk
rising to medium height
at which point appears a stumpy canopy of spiky needles

difficult_points: rising 是现在分词短语，修饰 trunk。
at which point 引导定语从句并发生倒装，真正的主语是 a stumpy canopy。

sentences.sentence: It's a tree, but a very special one.
sentences.translation: 这是一棵树，但却是一棵非常特别的树。

structure.text: It's a tree
but a very special one

difficult_points: but 后省略了 it is，形成简洁的转折结构。
one 代替前面的 tree，避免重复。

sentences.sentence: Ron Simonson, a park ranger explains.
sentences.translation: 公园护林员罗恩·西蒙森解释道。

structure.text: Ron Simonson
a park ranger
explains

difficult_points: a park ranger 是同位语，说明 Ron Simonson 的身份。
explains 后的解释内容放在下一句直接引语中。

sentences.sentence: "It's a bristlecone pine, and it's been given the name, Methuselah".
sentences.translation: “这是一棵狐尾松，人们给它取名为‘玛土撒拉’。”

structure.text: It's a bristlecone pine
and it's been given the name, Methuselah

difficult_points: and 连接两个并列分句，it 都指眼前这棵树。
has been given 是现在完成时的被动语态，强调名字已被赋予。

sentences.sentence: I ask the obvious question, and Ron replies, "Because like Methuselah from the bible, this tree is very old, one of the oldest living things on Earth in fact."
sentences.translation: 我问了那个显而易见的问题，罗恩回答：“因为它就像《圣经》中的玛土撒拉一样年迈；事实上，这棵树是地球上最古老的生物之一。”

structure.text: I ask the obvious question
and Ron replies
Because like Methuselah from the bible, this tree is very old, one of the oldest living things on Earth in fact

difficult_points: Because 回答被省略呈现的“为什么叫这个名字”这一问题。
one of the oldest 后接复数 living things，作 this tree 的补充说明。

expressions.text: one of the oldest living things

sentences.sentence: I ask the next obvious question, and Ron replies, "Basically Methuselah has existed throughout virtually all of recorded human history."
sentences.translation: 我又问了下一个显而易见的问题，罗恩回答：“基本上，有文字记载的人类历史几乎全程都有玛土撒拉存在。”

structure.text: I ask the next obvious question
and Ron replies
Basically Methuselah has existed throughout virtually all of recorded human history

difficult_points: has existed 使用现在完成时，表示其存在从远古延续至今。
throughout virtually all of 限定跨度，意为“几乎贯穿全部”。

expressions.meaning_zh: 贯穿有文字记载的人类历史

sentences.sentence: I look again at this quiet and unassuming tree, beginning to realise it is worthy of great respect.
sentences.translation: 我再次端详这棵安静而不起眼的树，开始意识到它值得人们深深敬重。

structure.text: I look again at this quiet and unassuming tree
beginning to realise
it is worthy of great respect

difficult_points: beginning 是现在分词，补充说明主语 I 同时发生的认识变化。
realise 后省略 that；it 指 this tree。

sentences.sentence: Being in a cold climate, facing limited summer seasons, rooted in nutrient poor and dry soil, and subject to high winds and withering winters, bristlecone pines mature very slowly indeed.
sentences.translation: 狐尾松生长在寒冷气候中，夏季短暂，扎根于贫瘠干燥的土壤，还要经受强风和严冬，因此成熟得极其缓慢。

structure.text: Being in a cold climate, facing limited summer seasons, rooted in nutrient poor and dry soil, and subject to high winds and withering winters
bristlecone pines mature very slowly indeed

difficult_points: 句首四个并列短语共同说明狐尾松的生长环境，逻辑主语都是 bristlecone pines。
rooted 与 subject to 描述被动处境，indeed 加强 slowly 的程度。

sentences.sentence: Yet mature they do, as with all pines becoming fractionally thicker every year as another growth ring is added to their truck.
sentences.translation: 然而，它们确实会成熟；所有松树都一样，随着树干每年增加一圈年轮，树身会稍稍变粗。

structure.text: Yet mature they do
as with all pines becoming fractionally thicker every year
as another growth ring is added to their truck

difficult_points: mature 被前置，do 用于强调，正常语序为 they do mature。
第二个 as 引导过程背景，is added 是被动语态；truck 是源文对 trunk 的拼写异常。

sentences.sentence: By counting these, we can accurately state that, as of 2011, Methuselah was 4,842 years old, meaning that it sprouted as a seedling in 2832 BC, centuries before the ancient Egyptians began building their pyramids.
sentences.translation: 通过数这些年轮，我们可以准确断定，截至2011年，玛土撒拉已有4842岁；这意味着它在公元前2832年便以幼苗形态萌发，比古埃及人开始建造金字塔早了几个世纪。

structure.text: By counting these, we can accurately state
that, as of 2011, Methuselah was 4,842 years old
meaning that it sprouted as a seedling in 2832 BC
centuries before the ancient Egyptians began building their pyramids

difficult_points: these 回指年轮；By counting 表示得出结论的方法。
meaning 是现在分词，说明4842岁这一数据所意味着的结果；before 从句进一步比较年代。

sentences.sentence: And that's just one fascinating fact about that well-known species of tree - the pine.
sentences.translation: 而这仅仅是关于那种著名树种——松树——的一个迷人事实。

structure.text: that's just one fascinating fact
about that well-known species of tree
the pine

difficult_points: that 回指上一句关于玛土撒拉树龄的信息。
破折号后的 the pine 与 that well-known species of tree 构成同位关系。

sentences.sentence: Pines trees are native to most of the Northern Hemisphere.
sentences.translation: 松树原生于北半球大部分地区。

structure.text: Pines trees
are native to
most of the Northern Hemisphere

difficult_points: be native to 表示某物原产于某地。
Pines trees 是源文中名词形式重复的异常表达。

sentences.sentence: Several species have adapted to the harsh conditions of high elevations and latitudes, including Methuselah himself, growing among the peaks of the White Mountains of Northern California.
sentences.translation: 一些松树物种已适应高海拔和高纬度的严酷环境，其中也包括生长在北加州怀特山峰峦间的玛土撒拉。

structure.text: Several species have adapted to the harsh conditions of high elevations and latitudes
including Methuselah himself
growing among the peaks of the White Mountains of Northern California

difficult_points: have adapted 使用现在完成时，表示适应结果延续至今。
growing 是现在分词短语，修饰 Methuselah himself。

sentences.sentence: Pines can be small, such as the Siberian Dwarf Pine, or huge, such as the Ponderosa Pine in the wilds of Oregon, and there are over 100 varieties in all.
sentences.translation: 松树可以很矮小，如西伯利亚矮松；也可以十分高大，如俄勒冈荒野中的西黄松；全世界共有100多个品种。

structure.text: Pines can be small, such as the Siberian Dwarf Pine, or huge, such as the Ponderosa Pine in the wilds of Oregon
and there are over 100 varieties in all

difficult_points: small 与 huge 是并列表语，各自用 such as 引出例子。
and 后是 there be 存现句，in all 表示总计。

sentences.sentence: They have been introduced in to the more temperate portions of the Southern Hemisphere, where they are now grown widely, becoming a familiar feature in parks and gardens.
sentences.translation: 松树也被引入南半球较为温和的地区，如今在那里广泛种植，成为公园和花园中常见的景观。

structure.text: They have been introduced in to the more temperate portions of the Southern Hemisphere
where they are now grown widely
becoming a familiar feature in parks and gardens

difficult_points: where 指代南半球较温和的地区，are grown 是被动语态。
becoming 是现在分词，表示广泛种植带来的结果；in to 是源文分写。

sentences.sentence: It would not be too much of an exaggeration to say that almost everyone knows pines.
sentences.translation: 说几乎人人都认识松树，并不算太夸张。

structure.text: It would not be too much of an exaggeration
to say
that almost everyone knows pines

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，to say... 是真正主语。
that 引导 say 的宾语从句，说明所说的具体内容。

expressions.text: not too much of an exaggeration

sentences.sentence: These trees certainly have many telltale characteristics.
sentences.translation: 这些树确实具有许多一望便知的特征。

structure.text: These trees
certainly have
many telltale characteristics

difficult_points: These trees 回指松树。
telltale 表示能清楚揭示身份或性质的典型特征。

sentences.sentence: They are evergreen, usually with needle-like foliage and a sharp pleasant 'pine smell.
sentences.translation: 它们是常绿树，通常长着针状叶片，并带有强烈而宜人的“松香味”。

structure.text: They are evergreen
with needle-like foliage and a sharp pleasant 'pine smell

difficult_points: with 短语补充说明常绿松树的叶片和气味特征。
pine smell 前只有左引号是源文标点异常。

sentences.sentence: They are often large and imposing, with thick scaly bark, and always produce their signature pine cones.
sentences.translation: 它们通常高大雄伟，树皮厚而呈鳞片状，并且总会结出标志性的松果。

structure.text: They are often large and imposing
with thick scaly bark
and always produce their signature pine cones

difficult_points: are 与 produce 是同一主语的并列谓语。
with thick scaly bark 补充树皮特征，signature 表示最具代表性的。

sentences.sentence: These formations are certainly not simple.
sentences.translation: 这些结构绝非简单。

structure.text: These formations
are
certainly not simple

difficult_points: These formations 回指上一句的 pine cones。
certainly not 加强否定语气，承接后文对松果复杂性的说明。

sentences.sentence: They can be male (small, inconspicuous, and shedding pollen) or female (large, woody, and containing seeds), even when appearing on the same tree.
sentences.translation: 松果可分为雄性（小而不起眼，会散播花粉）或雌性（较大、木质且含有种子），两者甚至会出现在同一棵树上。

structure.text: They can be male (small, inconspicuous, and shedding pollen) or female (large, woody, and containing seeds)
even when appearing on the same tree

difficult_points: male 与 female 是并列表语，括号分别解释两类松果。
when appearing 省略了 they are，逻辑主语为两类松果。

sentences.sentence: They have numerous scales arranged in a spiral, with seeds (on the female) tucked within.
sentences.translation: 松果有许多呈螺旋排列的鳞片，雌松果的种子藏在鳞片内部。

structure.text: They have numerous scales
arranged in a spiral
with seeds (on the female) tucked within

difficult_points: arranged 是过去分词短语，修饰 scales。
with + seeds + tucked 构成复合结构，表示种子被藏在里面。

sentences.sentence: As the cone opens, the seeds eventually fall out, mostly to be dispersed by the wind, or sometimes by birds.
sentences.translation: 随着松果张开，种子最终脱落，主要由风传播，有时也由鸟类传播。

structure.text: As the cone opens
the seeds eventually fall out
mostly to be dispersed by the wind, or sometimes by birds

difficult_points: As 引导时间或过程从句，表示松果打开的同时。
to be dispersed 是被动不定式，by the wind 与 by birds 是并列传播者。

sentences.sentence: In some varieties, the cones remain closed until their binding resin is melted by forest fires.
sentences.translation: 在某些品种中，松果会一直闭合，直到黏合它们的树脂被森林大火熔化。

structure.text: the cones remain closed
until their binding resin is melted by forest fires

difficult_points: remain 是系动词，closed 作表语说明持续状态。
until 引导时间从句，is melted 是被动语态。

sentences.sentence: This last fact - the need for wildfires for regeneration - is another fascinating aspect of many pine species.
sentences.translation: 最后这个事实——松树再生需要野火——是许多松树物种又一个引人入胜的特点。

structure.text: This last fact
the need for wildfires for regeneration
is another fascinating aspect of many pine species

difficult_points: 破折号中的名词短语与 This last fact 同位，说明所指事实。
两个 for 短语分别说明需要的对象和野火的用途。

sentences.sentence: In fire-prone areas, it can result in extensive stands of pines, a good example being in 'pine barrens'.
sentences.translation: 在易发生火灾的地区，这会形成大片松林，一个典型例子就是“松树荒原”。

structure.text: it can result in extensive stands of pines
a good example being in 'pine barrens'

difficult_points: it 回指野火对松树再生的作用。
a good example being... 是独立主格结构，补充举例。

sentences.sentence: These are eco-regions of sandy nutrient-poor soil dominated by pines, since the frequency of natural (usually lightning-induced) fires weeds out the less fire-tolerant species.
sentences.translation: 这些地区是由松树占优势、土壤沙质而贫瘠的生态区，因为频繁发生的天然火灾（通常由闪电引起）会淘汰耐火性较差的物种。

structure.text: These are eco-regions of sandy nutrient-poor soil dominated by pines
since the frequency of natural (usually lightning-induced) fires weeds out the less fire-tolerant species

difficult_points: dominated by pines 是过去分词短语，修饰 eco-regions。
since 引导原因从句；lightning-induced 与 fire-tolerant 都是复合形容词。

sentences.sentence: It is perhaps sad that modern fire prevention methods have resulted in the decline of many pine species in the wild, and most ancient pine barrens are now being taken over by other forest vegetation.
sentences.translation: 令人遗憾的是，现代防火手段导致许多野生松树物种衰退，而大多数古老松树荒原如今正被其他森林植被占据。

structure.text: It is perhaps sad
that modern fire prevention methods have resulted in the decline of many pine species in the wild
and most ancient pine barrens are now being taken over by other forest vegetation

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，that 从句是真正主语。
are being taken over 是现在进行时的被动语态，强调正在发生的替代。

sentences.sentence: However, the situation is very different for home and commercial use, which has seen pines become a very common sight.
sentences.translation: 然而，在家庭和商业用途方面，情况截然不同；这些用途使松树变得十分常见。

structure.text: the situation is very different for home and commercial use
which has seen pines become a very common sight

difficult_points: which 指代前面关于家庭和商业用途的整体情况。
see + object + bare infinitive 构成宾补结构，表示见证松树变得常见。

sentences.sentence: As these trees grow fast, can be planted in dense arrays, and produce attractive and easily moulded wood, they are favourites for commercial plantations.
sentences.translation: 由于这些树生长迅速、可密集种植，又能产出美观且易塑形的木材，因此很受商业种植园青睐。

structure.text: As these trees grow fast, can be planted in dense arrays, and produce attractive and easily moulded wood
they are favourites for commercial plantations

difficult_points: As 引导原因状语从句，内部有三个共享主语的并列谓语。
can be planted 是被动结构；moulded 是过去分词形容词，修饰 wood。

sentences.sentence: The wood is fragrant, but prone to decay, so it is most suitable for indoor or dry carpentry, rather than outdoors, where more durable varieties are necessary.
sentences.translation: 这种木材气味芳香，但容易腐朽，因此最适合室内或干燥环境下的木工作业，而不适合户外；户外需要更耐用的木材品种。

structure.text: The wood is fragrant, but prone to decay
so it is most suitable for indoor or dry carpentry, rather than outdoors
where more durable varieties are necessary

difficult_points: but 连接 fragrant 与 prone to decay 两个对照表语。
where 指代 outdoors，说明户外环境需要更耐用的品种。

sentences.sentence: As for other uses of pines, their branches are valued as Christmas trees, and their wood is also pulped in factories for paper and chipboard production.
sentences.translation: 至于松树的其他用途，其枝条可用作圣诞树，其木材也会在工厂制成纸浆，用于生产纸张和刨花板。

structure.text: their branches are valued as Christmas trees
and their wood is also pulped in factories
for paper and chipboard production

difficult_points: As for 用于转换话题，引出其他用途。
are valued 与 is pulped 都是被动语态，说明不同用途。

sentences.sentence: Pine resin is a byproduct, and this is collected for distillation into turpentine, an important industrial solvent.
sentences.translation: 松脂是一种副产品，人们收集它并蒸馏成松节油——一种重要的工业溶剂。

structure.text: Pine resin is a byproduct
and this is collected for distillation into turpentine
an important industrial solvent

difficult_points: this 回指 Pine resin，is collected 是被动语态。
an important industrial solvent 与 turpentine 构成同位关系。

sentences.sentence: In a more homely sense, perhaps what people most like are the cones, the largest of which are regularly used by children and craft enthusiasts.
sentences.translation: 从更贴近日常生活的角度看，人们最喜欢的也许是松果，其中最大的松果经常被儿童和手工艺爱好者使用。

structure.text: what people most like
are the cones
the largest of which are regularly used by children and craft enthusiasts

difficult_points: what 引导主语从句，意为“人们最喜欢的东西”。
the largest of which 指这些松果中最大的那些，are used 是被动语态。

expressions.meaning_zh: 从更贴近日常生活的角度

sentences.sentence: With the widespread distribution of pines across the Northern Hemisphere, cones form part of the many traditional cultures there, where they are used for decorative purposes, fire starters, bird feeders, or just intriguing natural playthings for young children.
sentences.translation: 由于松树广泛分布于北半球，松果成为当地许多传统文化的一部分；人们用它们作装饰、引火物、喂鸟器，或仅仅当作幼儿有趣的天然玩具。

structure.text: With the widespread distribution of pines across the Northern Hemisphere
cones form part of the many traditional cultures there
where they are used for decorative purposes, fire starters, bird feeders, or just intriguing natural playthings for young children

difficult_points: With 短语交代松果进入传统文化的背景。
where 指代 there 所表示的北半球地区，they 回指 cones。

sentences.sentence: Many people lament that modern manufactured toys in the more affluent of these countries have displaced cones, although some Nordic communities still teach traditional 'cone-craft' in high schools.
sentences.translation: 许多人惋惜的是，在这些国家中较富裕的地区，现代工业玩具已经取代了松果；不过，一些北欧社区仍在中学教授传统“松果工艺”。

structure.text: Many people lament
that modern manufactured toys in the more affluent of these countries have displaced cones
although some Nordic communities still teach traditional 'cone-craft' in high schools

difficult_points: that 引导 lament 的宾语从句，说明人们惋惜的内容。
although 引导让步从句，指出传统在部分北欧社区仍被保留。

sentences.sentence: For some reason, I always come back to Methuselah.
sentences.translation: 不知为什么，我的思绪总会回到玛土撒拉。

structure.text: For some reason
I always come back to Methuselah

difficult_points: For some reason 表示叙述者无法明确解释原因。
come back to 在此是话题或思绪回到玛土撒拉，并非物理返回。

sentences.sentence: Ron tells me a story.
sentences.translation: 罗恩给我讲了一个故事。

structure.text: Ron
tells
me
a story

difficult_points: tell 后接双宾语，me 是间接宾语，a story 是直接宾语。
一般现在时用于叙事，使现场讲述更直接。

sentences.sentence: In 1964, a student was taking a coring sample from another bristlecone pine in the area.
sentences.translation: 1964年，一名学生正在从该地区的另一棵狐尾松中钻取树芯样本。

structure.text: a student was taking a coring sample
from another bristlecone pine in the area

difficult_points: was taking 使用过去进行时，交代故事发生时正在进行的动作。
from 短语说明树芯样本取自哪棵树。

sentences.sentence: His coring toll broke, so the tree was cut down to allow dating by an examination of a cross-section of its trunk.
sentences.translation: 他的取芯工具坏了，于是人们砍倒了这棵树，以便通过检查树干横截面来测定年代。

structure.text: His coring toll broke
so the tree was cut down
to allow dating by an examination of a cross-section of its trunk

difficult_points: so 连接原因与结果，was cut down 是被动语态。
to allow 表示砍树的目的；toll 是源文对 tool 的拼写异常。

sentences.sentence: Upon doing this, to the astonishment of all, 4,844 rings were counted, signifying that the tree was even older than Methuselah.
sentences.translation: 这样做之后，令所有人震惊的是，他们数出了4844圈年轮，这表明那棵树甚至比玛土撒拉还要古老。

structure.text: Upon doing this
4,844 rings were counted
signifying that the tree was even older than Methuselah

difficult_points: Upon doing 表示“一……之后”，this 指检查树干横截面。
signifying 是现在分词，说明数出4844圈年轮所证明的结果。

sentences.sentence: Ron smiles wryly at the thought.
sentences.translation: 想到这里，罗恩苦涩地笑了。

structure.text: Ron
smiles wryly
at the thought

difficult_points: wryly 表示笑容中带有讽刺、无奈或苦涩。
the thought 回指人类为测年而杀死更古老树木这件事。

sentences.sentence: 'We deliberately killed the oldest life on Earth.
sentences.translation: “我们蓄意杀死了地球上最古老的生命。

structure.text: We
deliberately killed
the oldest life on Earth

difficult_points: deliberately 强调砍树行为是有意识作出的决定。
句首单引号在本句未闭合，因为同一段直接引语延续到后文。

sentences.sentence: That's one reason why we keep the location of Methuselah a secret.
sentences.translation: 这正是我们对玛土撒拉的位置保密的原因之一。

structure.text: That's one reason
why we keep the location of Methuselah a secret

difficult_points: why 引导定语从句修饰 reason，解释保密原因。
keep + object + complement 结构中 a secret 是宾语补足语。

sentences.sentence: This tree is precious, and must be kept free from all human interference.'
sentences.translation: 这棵树无比珍贵，必须使它免受一切人为干扰。”

structure.text: This tree is precious
and must be kept free from all human interference

difficult_points: and 后省略重复主语 This tree，must be kept 是情态动词的被动结构。
keep free from 表示使某物免受某种影响，句末单引号结束前文直接引语。
